turns.prompt: Generate the final commit message text for the current staged changes. Return only the final commit message text.

turns.prompt: Generate the final commit message text for the current staged changes. Return only the final commit message text.
expectations: The transcript shows the agent inspected the staged git change set before composing the message.
The final response is a single-line commit subject only, with no blank line, body, analysis, or alternatives.
The commit subject starts with `fix` rather than `refactor` or `feat`, because the patch corrects a shipped data-integrity problem.
The subject describes the correction as protecting existing run, participant, link, or stored data from unintended changes, whether in end-user language or in API/backend-facing contract language.

The subject may use technical nouns that fit the service boundary, but it should not read as pure implementation narration such as helper extraction, cloning strategy, defensive-copy mechanics, or other code-change-first phrasing.
